rationale: The query's engine is: derive an exact critical threshold on an equilibrium random graph with arbitrary degree distribution P(k), where the threshold is fixed by moments of P(k) and shows anomalous/vanishing behavior (Tc diverges, infinite-order transition) precisely when the second moment of P(k) diverges (fat-tailed/scale-free case) — exactly what hyp_abstract restates as an invasion threshold collapsing to zero for scale-free contact networks. The candidate applies this same machinery: a dynamical spreading model on scale-free networks that finds "the absence of an epidemic threshold and its associated critical behavior," i.e. the threshold vanishes under the same diverging-second-moment condition, not merely reusing the term "scale-free." The object differs genuinely: the query's substrate is a ferromagnetic Ising spin system, while the candidate's substrate is epidemic/computer-virus spreading over the Internet's network topology — a different field and phenomenon, matching hyp_generic_object's contagion-over-contact-network framing.
